title: A Survey on Deep Generative 3D-aware Image Synthesis
title_zh: 深度生成 3D 感知图像合成综述
comment: Accepted to ACM Computing Surveys. Project page: https://weihaox.github.io/3D-aware-Gen
comments: Accepted to ACM Computing Surveys. Project page: https://weihaox.github.io/3D-aware-Gen
abstract: Recent years have seen remarkable progress in deep learning powered visual content creation. This includes deep generative 3D-aware image synthesis, which produces high-idelity images in a 3D-consistent manner while simultaneously capturing compact surfaces of objects from pure image collections without the need for any 3D supervision, thus bridging the gap between 2D imagery and 3D reality. The ield of computer vision has been recently captivated by the task of deep generative 3D-aware image synthesis, with hundreds of papers appearing in top-tier journals and conferences over the past few years (mainly the past two years), but there lacks a comprehensive survey of this remarkable and swift progress. Our survey aims to introduce new researchers to this topic, provide a useful reference for related works, and stimulate future research directions through our discussion section. Apart from the presented papers, we aim to constantly update the latest relevant papers along with corresponding implementations at https://weihaox.github.io/3D-aware-Gen.
summary: Recent years have seen remarkable progress in deep learning powered visual content creation. This includes deep generative 3D-aware image synthesis, which produces high-idelity images in a 3D-consistent manner while simultaneously capturing compact surfaces of objects from pure image collections without the need for any 3D supervision, thus bridging the gap between 2D imagery and 3D reality. The ield of computer vision has been recently captivated by the task of deep generative 3D-aware image synthesis, with hundreds of papers appearing in top-tier journals and conferences over the past few years (mainly the past two years), but there lacks a comprehensive survey of this remarkable and swift progress. Our survey aims to introduce new researchers to this topic, provide a useful reference for related works, and stimulate future research directions through our discussion section. Apart from the presented papers, we aim to constantly update the latest relevant papers along with corresponding implementations at https://weihaox.github.io/3D-aware-Gen.
abstract_zh: 近年来，深度学习驱动的视觉内容创作取得了显着进展。这包括深度生成 3D 感知图像合成，它以 3D 一致的方式生成高保真度图像，同时从纯图像集合中捕获物体的紧凑表面，无需任何 3D 监督，从而弥合 2D 图像和 3D 现实之间的差距。计算机视觉领域最近被深度生成 3D 感知图像合成任务所吸引，过去几年（主要是过去两年）在顶级期刊和会议上发表了数百篇论文，但缺乏对这一显着而快速进展的全面调查。我们的调查旨在向该主题介绍新的研究人员，为相关工作提供有用的参考，并通过我们的讨论部分激发未来的研究方向。除了所提出的论文之外，我们的目标是不断更新最新的相关论文以及相应的实现，网址为 https://weihaox.github.io/3D-aware-Gen。

AI.tldr: 本文综述了深度生成3D感知图像合成领域的最新进展，旨在为研究者提供全面参考并推动未来研究方向。
AI.motivation: 尽管过去两年该领域论文数量激增，但缺乏系统性综述，阻碍了新手入门和研究方向的深入探索。
AI.method: 通过梳理顶级会议期刊论文，分类总结3D感知图像合成的核心技术、挑战及解决方案，并维护动态更新的在线资源库。
AI.result: 构建了首个系统性综述框架，涵盖技术演进、典型方法、评估指标及开放问题，配套网站实时更新最新研究成果。
AI.conclusion: 该综述填补了领域空白，为后续研究提供了方法论指导，并指出多模态融合与物理仿真等方向为未来突破关键。

tldr: 本文综述了深度生成3D感知图像合成领域的最新进展，旨在为研究者提供全面参考并推动未来研究方向。
motivation: 尽管过去两年该领域论文数量激增，但缺乏系统性综述，阻碍了新手入门和研究方向的深入探索。
method: 通过梳理顶级会议期刊论文，分类总结3D感知图像合成的核心技术、挑战及解决方案，并维护动态更新的在线资源库。
result: 构建了首个系统性综述框架，涵盖技术演进、典型方法、评估指标及开放问题，配套网站实时更新最新研究成果。
conclusion: 该综述填补了领域空白，为后续研究提供了方法论指导，并指出多模态融合与物理仿真等方向为未来突破关键。
search_text: A Survey on Deep Generative 3D-aware Image Synthesis 深度生成 3D 感知图像合成综述 Recent years have seen remarkable progress in deep learning powered visual content creation. This includes deep generative 3D-aware image synthesis, which produces high-idelity images in a 3D-consistent manner while simultaneously capturing compact surfaces of objects from pure image collections without the need for any 3D supervision, thus bridging the gap between 2D imagery and 3D reality. The ield of computer vision has been recently captivated by the task of deep generative 3D-aware image synthesis, with hundreds of papers appearing in top-tier journals and conferences over the past few years (mainly the past two years), but there lacks a comprehensive survey of this remarkable and swift progress. Our survey aims to introduce new researchers to this topic, provide a useful reference for related works, and stimulate future research directions through our discussion section. Apart from the presented papers, we aim to constantly update the latest relevant papers along with corresponding implementations at https://weihaox.github.io/3D-aware-Gen. 近年来，深度学习驱动的视觉内容创作取得了显着进展。这包括深度生成 3D 感知图像合成，它以 3D 一致的方式生成高保真度图像，同时从纯图像集合中捕获物体的紧凑表面，无需任何 3D 监督，从而弥合 2D 图像和 3D 现实之间的差距。计算机视觉领域最近被深度生成 3D 感知图像合成任务所吸引，过去几年（主要是过去两年）在顶级期刊和会议上发表了数百篇论文，但缺乏对这一显着而快速进展的全面调查。我们的调查旨在向该主题介绍新的研究人员，为相关工作提供有用的参考，并通过我们的讨论部分激发未来的研究方向。除了所提出的论文之外，我们的目标是不断更新最新的相关论文以及相应的实现，网址为 https://weihaox.github.io/3D-aware-Gen。 本文综述了深度生成3D感知图像合成领域的最新进展，旨在为研究者提供全面参考并推动未来研究方向。 Weihao Xia,Jing-Hao Xue cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.GR

authors_text: Dawei Liang,Hang Su,Tarun Singh,Jay Mahadeokar,Shanil Puri,Jiedan Zhu,Edison Thomaz,Mike Seltzer
title: Dynamic Speech Endpoint Detection with Regression Targets
title_zh: 具有回归目标的动态语音端点检测
abstract: Interactive voice assistants have been widely used as input interfaces in various scenarios, e.g. on smart homes devices, wearables and on AR devices. Detecting the end of a speech query, i.e. speech end-pointing, is an important task for voice assistants to interact with users. Traditionally, speech end-pointing is based on pure classification methods along with arbitrary binary targets. In this paper, we propose a novel regression-based speech end-pointing model, which enables an end-pointer to adjust its detection behavior based on context of user queries. Specifically, we present a pause modeling method and show its effectiveness for dynamic end-pointing. Based on our experiments with vendor-collected smartphone and wearables speech queries, our strategy shows a better trade-off between endpointing latency and accuracy, compared to the traditional classification-based method. We further discuss the benefits of this model and generalization of the framework in the paper.
summary: Interactive voice assistants have been widely used as input interfaces in various scenarios, e.g. on smart homes devices, wearables and on AR devices. Detecting the end of a speech query, i.e. speech end-pointing, is an important task for voice assistants to interact with users. Traditionally, speech end-pointing is based on pure classification methods along with arbitrary binary targets. In this paper, we propose a novel regression-based speech end-pointing model, which enables an end-pointer to adjust its detection behavior based on context of user queries. Specifically, we present a pause modeling method and show its effectiveness for dynamic end-pointing. Based on our experiments with vendor-collected smartphone and wearables speech queries, our strategy shows a better trade-off between endpointing latency and accuracy, compared to the traditional classification-based method. We further discuss the benefits of this model and generalization of the framework in the paper.
abstract_zh: 交互式语音助手已被广泛用作各种场景的输入界面，例如：在智能家居设备、可穿戴设备和 AR 设备上。检测语音查询的结束，即语音终点指向，是语音助手与用户交互的一项重要任务。传统上，语音端点定位基于纯分类方法以及任意二进制目标。在本文中，我们提出了一种新颖的基于回归的语音端点模型，该模型使端点能够根据用户查询的上下文调整其检测行为。具体来说，我们提出了一种暂停建模方法，并展示了其对于动态终点的有效性。根据我们对供应商收集的智能手机和可穿戴设备语音查询的实验，与传统的基于分类的方法相比，我们的策略显示了端点延迟和准确性之间更好的权衡。我们在本文中进一步讨论了该模型的好处和框架的泛化。

AI.tldr: 提出一种基于回归目标的动态语音端点检测方法，以提高检测的准确性和响应速度。
AI.motivation: 传统分类方法在语音端点检测中存在延迟与准确率的权衡问题，难以适应不同用户查询的上下文变化，需改进模型以提升性能。
AI.method: 提出回归模型，结合上下文信息调整检测行为，并引入暂停建模方法，通过回归目标优化端点检测。
AI.result: 实验表明，该方法在智能手机和可穿戴设备的数据集上相比传统分类方法，在延迟与准确率的权衡上表现更优。
AI.conclusion: 回归模型能有效提升动态语音端点检测的适应性与性能，为语音助手交互提供更优解决方案。

Traditionally, speech end-pointing is based on pure classification methods along with arbitrary binary targets. authors_text: Xin Shu,Mengxuan Niu,Yi Zhang,Wei Luo,Renjie Zhou
title: Neural Architecture Search generated Phase Retrieval Net for Real-time Off-axis Quantitative Phase Imaging
title_zh: 神经架构搜索生成相位检索网络，用于实时离轴定量相位成像
abstract: In off-axis Quantitative Phase Imaging (QPI), artificial neural networks have been recently applied for phase retrieval with aberration compensation and phase unwrapping. However, the involved neural network architectures are largely unoptimized and inefficient with low inference speed, which hinders the realization of real-time imaging. Here, we propose a Neural Architecture Search (NAS) generated Phase Retrieval Net (NAS-PRNet) for accurate and fast phase retrieval. NAS-PRNet is an encoder-decoder style neural network, automatically found from a large neural network architecture search space through NAS. By modifying the differentiable NAS scheme from SparseMask, we learn the optimized skip connections through gradient descent. Specifically, we implement MobileNet-v2 as the encoder and define a synthesized loss that incorporates phase reconstruction loss and network sparsity loss. NAS-PRNet has achieved high-fidelity phase retrieval by achieving a peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) of 36.7 dB and a Structural SIMilarity (SSIM) of 86.6% as tested on interferograms of biological cells. Notably, NAS-PRNet achieves phase retrieval in only 31 ms, representing 15x speedup over the most recent Mamba-UNet with only a slightly lower phase retrieval accuracy.
summary: In off-axis Quantitative Phase Imaging (QPI), artificial neural networks have been recently applied for phase retrieval with aberration compensation and phase unwrapping. However, the involved neural network architectures are largely unoptimized and inefficient with low inference speed, which hinders the realization of real-time imaging. Here, we propose a Neural Architecture Search (NAS) generated Phase Retrieval Net (NAS-PRNet) for accurate and fast phase retrieval. NAS-PRNet is an encoder-decoder style neural network, automatically found from a large neural network architecture search space through NAS. By modifying the differentiable NAS scheme from SparseMask, we learn the optimized skip connections through gradient descent. Specifically, we implement MobileNet-v2 as the encoder and define a synthesized loss that incorporates phase reconstruction loss and network sparsity loss. NAS-PRNet has achieved high-fidelity phase retrieval by achieving a peak Signal-to-Noise Ratio (PSNR) of 36.7 dB and a Structural SIMilarity (SSIM) of 86.6% as tested on interferograms of biological cells. Notably, NAS-PRNet achieves phase retrieval in only 31 ms, representing 15x speedup over the most recent Mamba-UNet with only a slightly lower phase retrieval accuracy.
abstract_zh: 在离轴定量相位成像（QPI）中，人工神经网络最近已应用于具有像差补偿和相位展开的相位检索。然而，所涉及的神经网络架构很大程度上未优化且效率低下，推理速度低，这阻碍了实时成像的实现。在这里，我们提出了一种神经架构搜索（NAS）生成的相位检索网络（NAS-PRNet），用于准确、快速的相位检索。 NAS-PRNet 是一种编码器-解码器风格的神经网络，通过 NAS 从大型神经网络架构搜索空间中自动找到。通过修改 SparseMask 的可微 NAS 方案，我们通过梯度下降学习优化的跳跃连接。具体来说，我们实现 MobileNet-v2 作为编码器，并定义一个综合损失，其中包含相位重建损失和网络稀疏损失。 NAS-PRNet 通过生物细胞干涉图测试，峰值信噪比 (PSNR) 达到 36.7 dB，结构相似度 (SSIM) 达到 86.6%，从而实现了高保真相位恢复。值得注意的是，NAS-PRNet 仅用 31 毫秒即可实现相位检索，比最新的 Mamba-UNet 加速了 15 倍，但相位检索精度仅略低。

AI.tldr: 设计高效神经网络实现实时离轴定量相位成像中的快速高精度相位恢复
AI.motivation: 现有神经网络结构未优化导致相位恢复速度慢，难以满足实时成像需求
AI.method: 基于NAS自动搜索编码器-解码器结构，结合MobileNet-v2编码器与相位重建损失+稀疏性损失的合成损失函数
AI.result: 在生物细胞干涉图中实现36.7dB PSNR和86.6% SSIM，相位恢复速度达31ms（较Mamba-UNet快15倍）
AI.conclusion: NAS-PRNet在保持高精度的同时显著提升推理速度，适用于实时定量相位成像场景

authors_text: Huan Hua,Jun Yan,Xi Fang,Weiquan Huang,Huilin Yin,Wancheng Ge
title: Causal Information Bottleneck Boosts Adversarial Robustness of Deep Neural Network
title_zh: 因果信息瓶颈增强深度神经网络的对抗鲁棒性
abstract: The information bottleneck (IB) method is a feasible defense solution against adversarial attacks in deep learning. However, this method suffers from the spurious correlation, which leads to the limitation of its further improvement of adversarial robustness. In this paper, we incorporate the causal inference into the IB framework to alleviate such a problem. Specifically, we divide the features obtained by the IB method into robust features (content information) and non-robust features (style information) via the instrumental variables to estimate the causal effects. With the utilization of such a framework, the influence of non-robust features could be mitigated to strengthen the adversarial robustness. We make an analysis of the effectiveness of our proposed method. The extensive experiments in MNIST, FashionMNIST, and CIFAR-10 show that our method exhibits the considerable robustness against multiple adversarial attacks. Our code would be released.
summary: The information bottleneck (IB) method is a feasible defense solution against adversarial attacks in deep learning. However, this method suffers from the spurious correlation, which leads to the limitation of its further improvement of adversarial robustness. In this paper, we incorporate the causal inference into the IB framework to alleviate such a problem. Specifically, we divide the features obtained by the IB method into robust features (content information) and non-robust features (style information) via the instrumental variables to estimate the causal effects. With the utilization of such a framework, the influence of non-robust features could be mitigated to strengthen the adversarial robustness. We make an analysis of the effectiveness of our proposed method. The extensive experiments in MNIST, FashionMNIST, and CIFAR-10 show that our method exhibits the considerable robustness against multiple adversarial attacks. Our code would be released.
abstract_zh: 信息瓶颈（IB）方法是深度学习中针对对抗性攻击的一种可行的防御解决方案。然而，该方法存在虚假相关性，导致其对抗鲁棒性的进一步提高受到限制。在本文中，我们将因果推理纳入 IB 框架中以缓解这一问题。具体来说，我们通过工具变量将IB方法获得的特征分为鲁棒特征（内容信息）和非鲁棒特征（风格信息）来估计因果效应。通过利用这样的框架，可以减轻非鲁棒特征的影响，以增强对抗性的鲁棒性。我们对我们提出的方法的有效性进行了分析。 MNIST、FashionMNIST 和 CIFAR-10 中的大量实验表明，我们的方法对多种对抗性攻击表现出相当的鲁棒性。我们的代码将被发布。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种结合因果推理的信息瓶颈方法，以提升深度神经网络对抗攻击的鲁棒性。
AI.motivation: 信息瓶颈方法在对抗攻击防御中存在伪相关性问题，限制了其进一步提升鲁棒性的效果。
AI.method: 通过工具变量估计因果效应，将信息瓶颈方法提取的特征分为鲁棒特征（内容信息）和非鲁棒特征（风格信息），抑制非鲁棒特征的影响。
AI.result: 在MNIST、FashionMNIST和CIFAR-10数据集上的实验表明，该方法对多种对抗攻击表现出显著的鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: 因果信息瓶颈框架通过消除伪相关性，有效增强了深度神经网络的对抗鲁棒性。

search_text: Causal Information Bottleneck Boosts Adversarial Robustness of Deep Neural Network 因果信息瓶颈增强深度神经网络的对抗鲁棒性 The information bottleneck (IB) method is a feasible defense solution against adversarial attacks in deep learning. However, this method suffers from the spurious correlation, which leads to the limitation of its further improvement of adversarial robustness. In this paper, we incorporate the causal inference into the IB framework to alleviate such a problem. Specifically, we divide the features obtained by the IB method into robust features (content information) and non-robust features (style information) via the instrumental variables to estimate the causal effects. With the utilization of such a framework, the influence of non-robust features could be mitigated to strengthen the adversarial robustness. We make an analysis of the effectiveness of our proposed method. The extensive experiments in MNIST, FashionMNIST, and CIFAR-10 show that our method exhibits the considerable robustness against multiple adversarial attacks. Our code would be released. 信息瓶颈（IB）方法是深度学习中针对对抗性攻击的一种可行的防御解决方案。然而，该方法存在虚假相关性，导致其对抗鲁棒性的进一步提高受到限制。在本文中，我们将因果推理纳入 IB 框架中以缓解这一问题。具体来说，我们通过工具变量将IB方法获得的特征分为鲁棒特征（内容信息）和非鲁棒特征（风格信息）来估计因果效应。通过利用这样的框架，可以减轻非鲁棒特征的影响，以增强对抗性的鲁棒性。我们对我们提出的方法的有效性进行了分析。 MNIST、FashionMNIST 和 CIFAR-10 中的大量实验表明，我们的方法对多种对抗性攻击表现出相当的鲁棒性。我们的代码将被发布。 本文提出了一种结合因果推理的信息瓶颈方法，以提升深度神经网络对抗攻击的鲁棒性。 Huan Hua,Jun Yan,Xi Fang,Weiquan Huang,Huilin Yin,Wancheng Ge cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.CR

authors_text: Christian Strack,Kelsey L. Pomykala,Heinz-Peter Schlemmer,Jan Egger,Jens Kleesiek
title: 'A net for everyone': fully personalized and unsupervised neural networks trained with longitudinal data from a single patient
title_zh: “适合每个人的网络”：完全个性化和无监督的神经网络，使用来自单个患者的纵向数据进行训练
abstract: With the rise in importance of personalized medicine, we trained personalized neural networks to detect tumor progression in longitudinal datasets. The model was evaluated on two datasets with a total of 64 scans from 32 patients diagnosed with glioblastoma multiforme (GBM). Contrast-enhanced T1w sequences of brain magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) images were used in this study. For each patient, we trained their own neural network using just two images from different timepoints. Our approach uses a Wasserstein-GAN (generative adversarial network), an unsupervised network architecture, to map the differences between the two images. Using this map, the change in tumor volume can be evaluated. Due to the combination of data augmentation and the network architecture, co-registration of the two images is not needed. Furthermore, we do not rely on any additional training data, (manual) annotations or pre-training neural networks. The model received an AUC-score of 0.87 for tumor change. We also introduced a modified RANO criteria, for which an accuracy of 66% can be achieved. We show that using data from just one patient can be used to train deep neural networks to monitor tumor change.
summary: With the rise in importance of personalized medicine, we trained personalized neural networks to detect tumor progression in longitudinal datasets. The model was evaluated on two datasets with a total of 64 scans from 32 patients diagnosed with glioblastoma multiforme (GBM). Contrast-enhanced T1w sequences of brain magnetic resonance imaging (MRI) images were used in this study. For each patient, we trained their own neural network using just two images from different timepoints. Our approach uses a Wasserstein-GAN (generative adversarial network), an unsupervised network architecture, to map the differences between the two images. Using this map, the change in tumor volume can be evaluated. Due to the combination of data augmentation and the network architecture, co-registration of the two images is not needed. Furthermore, we do not rely on any additional training data, (manual) annotations or pre-training neural networks. The model received an AUC-score of 0.87 for tumor change. We also introduced a modified RANO criteria, for which an accuracy of 66% can be achieved. We show that using data from just one patient can be used to train deep neural networks to monitor tumor change.
abstract_zh: 随着个性化医疗重要性的上升，我们训练个性化神经网络来检测纵向数据集中的肿瘤进展。该模型在两个数据集上进行了评估，其中包括来自 32 名诊断为多形性胶质母细胞瘤 (GBM) 的患者的总共 64 次扫描。本研究使用脑磁共振成像 (MRI) 图像的对比增强 T1w 序列。对于每位患者，我们仅使用来自不同时间点的两张图像来训练他们自己的神经网络。我们的方法使用 Wasserstein-GAN（生成对抗网络）（一种无监督网络架构）来映射两个图像之间的差异。使用该图，可以评估肿瘤体积的变化。由于数据增强和网络架构的结合，不需要两个图像的共同配准。此外，我们不依赖任何额外的训练数据、（手动）注释或预训练神经网络。该模型的肿瘤变化 AUC 得分为 0.87。我们还引入了修改后的 RANO 标准，其准确率可以达到 66%。我们证明，仅使用一名患者的数据就可以训练深度神经网络来监测肿瘤变化。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于单个患者纵向数据的全个性化无监督神经网络方法，用于检测胶质母细胞瘤的肿瘤进展。
AI.motivation: 个性化医疗需求推动肿瘤监测技术发展，传统方法依赖大量标注数据或预训练模型，而临床实际中常面临数据稀缺问题。
AI.method: 采用Wasserstein-GAN架构，利用每位患者仅有的两次时间点的MRI图像进行无监督训练，通过图像差异映射评估肿瘤体积变化，无需图像配准或额外标注数据。
AI.result: 模型在肿瘤变化检测中达到0.87的AUC值，改进的RANO标准准确率达66%，验证了单患者数据训练深度网络的有效性。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为个性化肿瘤监测提供了新思路，证明在数据稀缺场景下可通过无监督学习实现有效肿瘤进展评估。

title: FedClassAvg: Local Representation Learning for Personalized Federated Learning on Heterogeneous Neural Networks
title_zh: FedClassAvg：异构神经网络上个性化联邦学习的本地表示学习
abstract: Personalized federated learning is aimed at allowing numerous clients to train personalized models while participating in collaborative training in a communication-efficient manner without exchanging private data. However, many personalized federated learning algorithms assume that clients have the same neural network architecture, and those for heterogeneous models remain understudied. In this study, we propose a novel personalized federated learning method called federated classifier averaging (FedClassAvg). Deep neural networks for supervised learning tasks consist of feature extractor and classifier layers. FedClassAvg aggregates classifier weights as an agreement on decision boundaries on feature spaces so that clients with not independently and identically distributed (non-iid) data can learn about scarce labels. In addition, local feature representation learning is applied to stabilize the decision boundaries and improve the local feature extraction capabilities for clients. While the existing methods require the collection of auxiliary data or model weights to generate a counterpart, FedClassAvg only requires clients to communicate with a couple of fully connected layers, which is highly communication-efficient. Moreover, FedClassAvg does not require extra optimization problems such as knowledge transfer, which requires intensive computation overhead. We evaluated FedClassAvg through extensive experiments and demonstrated it outperforms the current state-of-the-art algorithms on heterogeneous personalized federated learning tasks.
summary: Personalized federated learning is aimed at allowing numerous clients to train personalized models while participating in collaborative training in a communication-efficient manner without exchanging private data. However, many personalized federated learning algorithms assume that clients have the same neural network architecture, and those for heterogeneous models remain understudied. In this study, we propose a novel personalized federated learning method called federated classifier averaging (FedClassAvg). Deep neural networks for supervised learning tasks consist of feature extractor and classifier layers. FedClassAvg aggregates classifier weights as an agreement on decision boundaries on feature spaces so that clients with not independently and identically distributed (non-iid) data can learn about scarce labels. In addition, local feature representation learning is applied to stabilize the decision boundaries and improve the local feature extraction capabilities for clients. While the existing methods require the collection of auxiliary data or model weights to generate a counterpart, FedClassAvg only requires clients to communicate with a couple of fully connected layers, which is highly communication-efficient. Moreover, FedClassAvg does not require extra optimization problems such as knowledge transfer, which requires intensive computation overhead. We evaluated FedClassAvg through extensive experiments and demonstrated it outperforms the current state-of-the-art algorithms on heterogeneous personalized federated learning tasks.
abstract_zh: 个性化联邦学习旨在允许众多客户训练个性化模型，同时以高效通信的方式参与协作训练，而无需交换私有数据。然而，许多个性化联邦学习算法假设客户端具有相同的神经网络架构，而异构模型的算法仍未得到充分研究。在这项研究中，我们提出了一种新颖的个性化联邦学习方法，称为联邦分类器平均（FedClassAvg）。用于监督学习任务的深度神经网络由特征提取器和分类器层组成。 FedClassAvg 将分类器权重聚合为特征空间上决策边界的协议，以便具有非独立同分布（非独立同分布）数据的客户端可以了解稀缺标签。此外，应用局部特征表示学习来稳定决策边界并提高客户端的局部特征提取能力。现有方法需要收集辅助数据或模型权重来生成对应数据，而 FedClassAvg 仅需要客户端与几个完全连接的层进行通信，通信效率很高。此外，FedClassAvg 不需要额外的优化问题，例如知识转移，这需要大量的计算开销。我们通过大量实验评估了 FedClassAvg，并证明它在异构个性化联邦学习任务上优于当前最先进的算法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为FedClassAvg的个性化联邦学习方法，旨在解决异构神经网络在非独立同分布数据下的个性化模型训练问题。
AI.motivation: 现有个性化联邦学习算法假设客户端具有相同网络结构，难以处理异构模型；同时多数方法需要额外数据或计算开销，缺乏通信效率。
AI.method: FedClassAvg通过聚合分类器权重对齐特征空间决策边界，并结合本地特征表示学习提升特征提取能力，仅需共享全连接层参数且无需知识迁移等额外优化步骤。
AI.result: 实验表明FedClassAvg在异构个性化联邦学习任务中优于现有最先进算法，且通信效率更高。
AI.conclusion: FedClassAvg为异构神经网络的个性化联邦学习提供了高效解决方案，在保持通信效率的同时提升了模型性能。

search_text: FedClassAvg: Local Representation Learning for Personalized Federated Learning on Heterogeneous Neural Networks FedClassAvg：异构神经网络上个性化联邦学习的本地表示学习 Personalized federated learning is aimed at allowing numerous clients to train personalized models while participating in collaborative training in a communication-efficient manner without exchanging private data. However, many personalized federated learning algorithms assume that clients have the same neural network architecture, and those for heterogeneous models remain understudied. In this study, we propose a novel personalized federated learning method called federated classifier averaging (FedClassAvg). Deep neural networks for supervised learning tasks consist of feature extractor and classifier layers. FedClassAvg aggregates classifier weights as an agreement on decision boundaries on feature spaces so that clients with not independently and identically distributed (non-iid) data can learn about scarce labels. In addition, local feature representation learning is applied to stabilize the decision boundaries and improve the local feature extraction capabilities for clients. While the existing methods require the collection of auxiliary data or model weights to generate a counterpart, FedClassAvg only requires clients to communicate with a couple of fully connected layers, which is highly communication-efficient. Moreover, FedClassAvg does not require extra optimization problems such as knowledge transfer, which requires intensive computation overhead. We evaluated FedClassAvg through extensive experiments and demonstrated it outperforms the current state-of-the-art algorithms on heterogeneous personalized federated learning tasks. 个性化联邦学习旨在允许众多客户训练个性化模型，同时以高效通信的方式参与协作训练，而无需交换私有数据。然而，许多个性化联邦学习算法假设客户端具有相同的神经网络架构，而异构模型的算法仍未得到充分研究。在这项研究中，我们提出了一种新颖的个性化联邦学习方法，称为联邦分类器平均（FedClassAvg）。用于监督学习任务的深度神经网络由特征提取器和分类器层组成。 FedClassAvg 将分类器权重聚合为特征空间上决策边界的协议，以便具有非独立同分布（非独立同分布）数据的客户端可以了解稀缺标签。此外，应用局部特征表示学习来稳定决策边界并提高客户端的局部特征提取能力。现有方法需要收集辅助数据或模型权重来生成对应数据，而 FedClassAvg 仅需要客户端与几个完全连接的层进行通信，通信效率很高。此外，FedClassAvg 不需要额外的优化问题，例如知识转移，这需要大量的计算开销。我们通过大量实验评估了 FedClassAvg，并证明它在异构个性化联邦学习任务上优于当前最先进的算法。 本文提出了一种名为FedClassAvg的个性化联邦学习方法，旨在解决异构神经网络在非独立同分布数据下的个性化模型训练问题。 Jaehee Jang,Heonseok Ha,Dahuin Jung,Sungroh Yoon cs.LG,cs.AI,cs.DC

title: Flexible Android Malware Detection Model based on Generative Adversarial Networks with Code Tensor
title_zh: 基于代码张量生成对抗网络的灵活 Android 恶意软件检测模型
abstract: The behavior of malware threats is gradually increasing, heightened the need for malware detection. However, existing malware detection methods only target at the existing malicious samples, the detection of fresh malicious code and variants of malicious code is limited. In this paper, we propose a novel scheme that detects malware and its variants efficiently. Based on the idea of the generative adversarial networks (GANs), we obtain the `true' sample distribution that satisfies the characteristics of the real malware, use them to deceive the discriminator, thus achieve the defense against malicious code attacks and improve malware detection. Firstly, a new Android malware APK to image texture feature extraction segmentation method is proposed, which is called segment self-growing texture segmentation algorithm. Secondly, tensor singular value decomposition (tSVD) based on the low-tubal rank transforms malicious features with different sizes into a fixed third-order tensor uniformly, which is entered into the neural network for training and learning. Finally, a flexible Android malware detection model based on GANs with code tensor (MTFD-GANs) is proposed. Experiments show that the proposed model can generally surpass the traditional malware detection model, with a maximum improvement efficiency of 41.6\%. At the same time, the newly generated samples of the GANs generator greatly enrich the sample diversity. And retraining malware detector can effectively improve the detection efficiency and robustness of traditional models.
summary: The behavior of malware threats is gradually increasing, heightened the need for malware detection. However, existing malware detection methods only target at the existing malicious samples, the detection of fresh malicious code and variants of malicious code is limited. In this paper, we propose a novel scheme that detects malware and its variants efficiently. Based on the idea of the generative adversarial networks (GANs), we obtain the `true' sample distribution that satisfies the characteristics of the real malware, use them to deceive the discriminator, thus achieve the defense against malicious code attacks and improve malware detection. Firstly, a new Android malware APK to image texture feature extraction segmentation method is proposed, which is called segment self-growing texture segmentation algorithm. Secondly, tensor singular value decomposition (tSVD) based on the low-tubal rank transforms malicious features with different sizes into a fixed third-order tensor uniformly, which is entered into the neural network for training and learning. Finally, a flexible Android malware detection model based on GANs with code tensor (MTFD-GANs) is proposed. Experiments show that the proposed model can generally surpass the traditional malware detection model, with a maximum improvement efficiency of 41.6\%. At the same time, the newly generated samples of the GANs generator greatly enrich the sample diversity. And retraining malware detector can effectively improve the detection efficiency and robustness of traditional models.
abstract_zh: 恶意软件威胁的行为正在逐渐增加，增加了恶意软件检测的需求。然而，现有的恶意软件检测方法仅针对现有的恶意样本，对新鲜恶意代码和恶意代码变种的检测有限。在本文中，我们提出了一种有效检测恶意软件及其变体的新颖方案。基于生成对抗网络（GAN）的思想，我们获得满足真实恶意软件特征的“真实”样本分布，利用它们来欺骗鉴别器，从而实现防御恶意代码攻击并提高恶意软件检测。首先，提出了一种新的Android恶意软件APK图像纹理特征提取分割方法，称为分段自生长纹理分割算法。其次，基于低管秩的张量奇异值分解（tSVD）将不同大小的恶意特征统一转化为固定的三阶张量，输入神经网络进行训练和学习。最后，提出了一种基于带有代码张量的 GAN（MTFD-GAN）的灵活 Android 恶意软件检测模型。实验表明，所提出的模型总体上可以超越传统的恶意软件检测模型，最高提升效率达到41.6%。同时，GANs生成器新生成的样本极大地丰富了样本多样性。并且重新训练恶意软件检测器可以有效提高传统模型的检测效率和鲁棒性。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种基于生成对抗网络（GANs）和代码张量的灵活Android恶意软件检测模型MTFD-GANs，以提高对新型恶意代码及变种的检测能力。
AI.motivation: 现有恶意软件检测方法难以有效识别新型恶意代码及变种，亟需提升检测效率与模型鲁棒性。
AI.method: 提出分段自增长纹理分割算法提取APK图像特征，结合张量奇异值分解（tSVD）统一特征维度，并构建基于代码张量的GANs检测模型。
AI.result: 实验表明，该模型相较传统方法检测效率提升最高达41.6%，生成样本显著增强模型多样性与鲁棒性。
AI.conclusion: MTFD-GANs模型通过生成对抗机制与张量特征融合，有效提升了Android恶意软件检测的准确性与适应性。

authors_text: Katrin Renz,Kashyap Chitta,Otniel-Bogdan Mercea,A. Sophia Koepke,Zeynep Akata,Andreas Geiger
title: PlanT: Explainable Planning Transformers via Object-Level Representations
title_zh: PlanT：通过对象级表示的可解释规划变压器
abstract: Planning an optimal route in a complex environment requires efficient reasoning about the surrounding scene. While human drivers prioritize important objects and ignore details not relevant to the decision, learning-based planners typically extract features from dense, high-dimensional grid representations containing all vehicle and road context information. In this paper, we propose PlanT, a novel approach for planning in the context of self-driving that uses a standard transformer architecture. PlanT is based on imitation learning with a compact object-level input representation. On the Longest6 benchmark for CARLA, PlanT outperforms all prior methods (matching the driving score of the expert) while being 5.3x faster than equivalent pixel-based planning baselines during inference. Combining PlanT with an off-the-shelf perception module provides a sensor-based driving system that is more than 10 points better in terms of driving score than the existing state of the art. Furthermore, we propose an evaluation protocol to quantify the ability of planners to identify relevant objects, providing insights regarding their decision-making. Our results indicate that PlanT can focus on the most relevant object in the scene, even when this object is geometrically distant.
summary: Planning an optimal route in a complex environment requires efficient reasoning about the surrounding scene. While human drivers prioritize important objects and ignore details not relevant to the decision, learning-based planners typically extract features from dense, high-dimensional grid representations containing all vehicle and road context information. In this paper, we propose PlanT, a novel approach for planning in the context of self-driving that uses a standard transformer architecture. PlanT is based on imitation learning with a compact object-level input representation. On the Longest6 benchmark for CARLA, PlanT outperforms all prior methods (matching the driving score of the expert) while being 5.3x faster than equivalent pixel-based planning baselines during inference. Combining PlanT with an off-the-shelf perception module provides a sensor-based driving system that is more than 10 points better in terms of driving score than the existing state of the art. Furthermore, we propose an evaluation protocol to quantify the ability of planners to identify relevant objects, providing insights regarding their decision-making. Our results indicate that PlanT can focus on the most relevant object in the scene, even when this object is geometrically distant.
abstract_zh: 在复杂环境中规划最佳路线需要对周围场景进行有效推理。虽然人类驾驶员优先考虑重要的物体并忽略与决策无关的细节，但基于学习的规划器通常从包含所有车辆和道路上下文信息的密集、高维网格表示中提取特征。在本文中，我们提出了 PlanT，这是一种在自动驾驶背景下使用标准变压器架构进行规划的新颖方法。 PlanT 基于具有紧凑对象级输入表示的模仿学习。在 CARLA 的 Longest6 基准上，PlanT 优于所有现有方法（与专家的驾驶分数匹配），同时在推理过程中比同等的基于像素的规划基线快 5.3 倍。将 PlanT 与现成的感知模块相结合，可提供基于传感器的驾驶系统，其驾驶分数比现有技术高出 10 分以上。此外，我们提出了一个评估协议来量化规划者识别相关对象的能力，从而提供有关他们决策的见解。我们的结果表明，PlanT 可以聚焦于场景中最相关的对象，即使该对象在几何上较远。

AI.tldr: 本文提出PlanT，一种基于对象级表示的可解释规划变压器模型，用于自动驾驶中的路径规划任务。
AI.motivation: 传统基于像素的规划方法计算效率低且难以解释，而人类驾驶员会关注关键对象。本文旨在开发一种更高效且可解释的规划方法。
AI.method: 采用模仿学习框架，使用紧凑的对象级输入表示替代密集网格，结合标准Transformer架构，并设计评估协议量化规划器对关键对象的识别能力。
AI.result: 在Longest6基准测试中，PlanT的驾驶评分与专家相当，推理速度比像素级方法快5.3倍；结合感知模块后驾驶评分超越现有方法10分以上。
AI.conclusion: PlanT通过对象级表示实现了高效可解释的规划，验证了关注关键对象的有效性，为自动驾驶系统提供了新思路。

search_text: PlanT: Explainable Planning Transformers via Object-Level Representations PlanT：通过对象级表示的可解释规划变压器 Planning an optimal route in a complex environment requires efficient reasoning about the surrounding scene. While human drivers prioritize important objects and ignore details not relevant to the decision, learning-based planners typically extract features from dense, high-dimensional grid representations containing all vehicle and road context information. In this paper, we propose PlanT, a novel approach for planning in the context of self-driving that uses a standard transformer architecture. PlanT is based on imitation learning with a compact object-level input representation. On the Longest6 benchmark for CARLA, PlanT outperforms all prior methods (matching the driving score of the expert) while being 5.3x faster than equivalent pixel-based planning baselines during inference. Combining PlanT with an off-the-shelf perception module provides a sensor-based driving system that is more than 10 points better in terms of driving score than the existing state of the art. Furthermore, we propose an evaluation protocol to quantify the ability of planners to identify relevant objects, providing insights regarding their decision-making. Our results indicate that PlanT can focus on the most relevant object in the scene, even when this object is geometrically distant. 在复杂环境中规划最佳路线需要对周围场景进行有效推理。虽然人类驾驶员优先考虑重要的物体并忽略与决策无关的细节，但基于学习的规划器通常从包含所有车辆和道路上下文信息的密集、高维网格表示中提取特征。在本文中，我们提出了 PlanT，这是一种在自动驾驶背景下使用标准变压器架构进行规划的新颖方法。 PlanT 基于具有紧凑对象级输入表示的模仿学习。在 CARLA 的 Longest6 基准上，PlanT 优于所有现有方法（与专家的驾驶分数匹配），同时在推理过程中比同等的基于像素的规划基线快 5.3 倍。将 PlanT 与现成的感知模块相结合，可提供基于传感器的驾驶系统，其驾驶分数比现有技术高出 10 分以上。此外，我们提出了一个评估协议来量化规划者识别相关对象的能力，从而提供有关他们决策的见解。我们的结果表明，PlanT 可以聚焦于场景中最相关的对象，即使该对象在几何上较远。 本文提出PlanT，一种基于对象级表示的可解释规划变压器模型，用于自动驾驶中的路径规划任务。 Katrin Renz,Kashyap Chitta,Otniel-Bogdan Mercea,A. Sophia Koepke,Zeynep Akata,Andreas Geiger cs.RO,cs.AI,cs.CV,cs.LG

authors_text: Pavol Harar,Dennis Elbrächter,Monika Dörfler,Kory D. Johnson
title: Redistributor: Transforming Empirical Data Distributions
title_zh: 再分配器：转变经验数据分布
comment: 16 pages, 13 figures - Added more use cases and comparisons with other methods
comments: 16 pages, 13 figures - Added more use cases and comparisons with other methods
abstract: We present an algorithm and package, Redistributor, which forces a collection of scalar samples to follow a desired distribution. When given independent and identically distributed samples of some random variable $S$ and the continuous cumulative distribution function of some desired target $T$, it provably produces a consistent estimator of the transformation $R$ which satisfies $R(S)=T$ in distribution. As the distribution of $S$ or $T$ may be unknown, we also include algorithms for efficiently estimating these distributions from samples. This allows for various interesting use cases in image processing, where Redistributor serves as a remarkably simple and easy-to-use tool that is capable of producing visually appealing results. For color correction it outperforms other model-based methods and excels in achieving photorealistic style transfer, surpassing deep learning methods in content preservation. The package is implemented in Python and is optimized to efficiently handle large datasets, making it also suitable as a preprocessing step in machine learning. The source code is available at https://github.com/paloha/redistributor.
summary: We present an algorithm and package, Redistributor, which forces a collection of scalar samples to follow a desired distribution. When given independent and identically distributed samples of some random variable $S$ and the continuous cumulative distribution function of some desired target $T$, it provably produces a consistent estimator of the transformation $R$ which satisfies $R(S)=T$ in distribution. As the distribution of $S$ or $T$ may be unknown, we also include algorithms for efficiently estimating these distributions from samples. This allows for various interesting use cases in image processing, where Redistributor serves as a remarkably simple and easy-to-use tool that is capable of producing visually appealing results. For color correction it outperforms other model-based methods and excels in achieving photorealistic style transfer, surpassing deep learning methods in content preservation. The package is implemented in Python and is optimized to efficiently handle large datasets, making it also suitable as a preprocessing step in machine learning. The source code is available at https://github.com/paloha/redistributor.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了一种算法和包 Redistributor，它强制标量样本的集合遵循所需的分布。当给定某个随机变量$S$的独立同分布样本和某个期望目标$T$的连续累积分布函数时，它可以证明产生变换$R$的一致估计量，其分布满足$R(S)=T$。由于 $S$ 或 $T$ 的分布可能未知，我们还包括用于从样本中有效估计这些分布的算法。这允许图像处理中的各种有趣的用例，其中 Redistributor 作为一个非常简单且易于使用的工具，能够产生视觉上吸引人的结果。在色彩校正方面，它优于其他基于模型的方法，并且擅长实现逼真的风格迁移，在内容保存方面超越了深度学习方法。该包是用 Python 实现的，并经过优化以有效处理大型数据集，使其也适合作为机器学习中的预处理步骤。源代码可在 https://github.com/paloha/redistributor 获取。

AI.tldr: 开发一种算法和工具包，将经验数据分布转换为指定目标分布。
AI.motivation: 解决在分布未知情况下如何将样本转换为目标分布的问题，并在图像处理中提供高效工具。
AI.method: 通过估计源和目标分布，构造转换函数R，使样本符合目标分布。
AI.result: 在颜色校正和风格迁移任务中优于现有方法，且处理大规模数据高效。
AI.conclusion: Redistributor提供了一种高效、易用的工具，适用于图像处理和机器学习预处理。

authors_text: Michael Laskin,Luyu Wang,Junhyuk Oh,Emilio Parisotto,Stephen Spencer,Richie Steigerwald,DJ Strouse,Steven Hansen,Angelos Filos,Ethan Brooks,Maxime Gazeau,Himanshu Sahni,Satinder Singh,Volodymyr Mnih
title: In-context Reinforcement Learning with Algorithm Distillation
title_zh: 通过算法蒸馏进行上下文强化学习
abstract: We propose Algorithm Distillation (AD), a method for distilling reinforcement learning (RL) algorithms into neural networks by modeling their training histories with a causal sequence model. Algorithm Distillation treats learning to reinforcement learn as an across-episode sequential prediction problem. A dataset of learning histories is generated by a source RL algorithm, and then a causal transformer is trained by autoregressively predicting actions given their preceding learning histories as context. Unlike sequential policy prediction architectures that distill post-learning or expert sequences, AD is able to improve its policy entirely in-context without updating its network parameters. We demonstrate that AD can reinforcement learn in-context in a variety of environments with sparse rewards, combinatorial task structure, and pixel-based observations, and find that AD learns a more data-efficient RL algorithm than the one that generated the source data.
summary: We propose Algorithm Distillation (AD), a method for distilling reinforcement learning (RL) algorithms into neural networks by modeling their training histories with a causal sequence model. Algorithm Distillation treats learning to reinforcement learn as an across-episode sequential prediction problem. A dataset of learning histories is generated by a source RL algorithm, and then a causal transformer is trained by autoregressively predicting actions given their preceding learning histories as context. Unlike sequential policy prediction architectures that distill post-learning or expert sequences, AD is able to improve its policy entirely in-context without updating its network parameters. We demonstrate that AD can reinforcement learn in-context in a variety of environments with sparse rewards, combinatorial task structure, and pixel-based observations, and find that AD learns a more data-efficient RL algorithm than the one that generated the source data.
abstract_zh: 我们提出了算法蒸馏（AD），一种通过使用因果序列模型对训练历史进行建模，将强化学习（RL）算法蒸馏到神经网络中的方法。算法蒸馏将强化学习的学习视为跨情节顺序预测问题。学习历史数据集由源 RL 算法生成，然后通过以之前的学习历史作为上下文，通过自回归预测动作来训练因果变换器。与提取学习后序列或专家序列的顺序策略预测架构不同，AD 能够完全在上下文中改进其策略，而无需更新其网络参数。我们证明 AD 可以在具有稀疏奖励、组合任务结构和基于像素的观察的各种环境中进行强化学习，并发现 AD 学习了一种比生成源数据的算法更高效的 RL 算法。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为算法蒸馏（AD）的方法，通过建模强化学习算法的训练历史，将RL算法蒸馏到神经网络中，实现上下文强化学习。
AI.motivation: 传统强化学习方法在稀疏奖励、复杂任务结构和像素观察等场景下数据效率较低，且需依赖参数更新。AD通过上下文学习直接提升策略，无需参数更新，从而提高数据效率。
AI.method: AD使用因果序列模型（因果变换器）建模源RL算法的训练历史，通过自回归预测动作，以学习历史作为上下文进行策略生成。
AI.result: AD在多种稀疏奖励、组合任务和像素观察环境中成功实现上下文强化学习，且比源算法更数据高效。
AI.conclusion: AD通过因果序列建模有效实现了上下文强化学习，为数据高效RL提供了新方法，尤其适用于复杂环境。

authors_text: Sudharshan Suresh,Zilin Si,Stuart Anderson,Michael Kaess,Mustafa Mukadam
title: MidasTouch: Monte-Carlo inference over distributions across sliding touch
title_zh: MidasTouch：滑动触摸分布的蒙特卡罗推理
abstract: We present MidasTouch, a tactile perception system for online global localization of a vision-based touch sensor sliding on an object surface. This framework takes in posed tactile images over time, and outputs an evolving distribution of sensor pose on the object's surface, without the need for visual priors. Our key insight is to estimate local surface geometry with tactile sensing, learn a compact representation for it, and disambiguate these signals over a long time horizon. The backbone of MidasTouch is a Monte-Carlo particle filter, with a measurement model based on a tactile code network learned from tactile simulation. This network, inspired by LIDAR place recognition, compactly summarizes local surface geometries. These generated codes are efficiently compared against a precomputed tactile codebook per-object, to update the pose distribution. We further release the YCB-Slide dataset of real-world and simulated forceful sliding interactions between a vision-based tactile sensor and standard YCB objects. While single-touch localization can be inherently ambiguous, we can quickly localize our sensor by traversing salient surface geometries. Project page: https://suddhu.github.io/midastouch-tactile/
summary: We present MidasTouch, a tactile perception system for online global localization of a vision-based touch sensor sliding on an object surface. This framework takes in posed tactile images over time, and outputs an evolving distribution of sensor pose on the object's surface, without the need for visual priors. Our key insight is to estimate local surface geometry with tactile sensing, learn a compact representation for it, and disambiguate these signals over a long time horizon. The backbone of MidasTouch is a Monte-Carlo particle filter, with a measurement model based on a tactile code network learned from tactile simulation. This network, inspired by LIDAR place recognition, compactly summarizes local surface geometries. These generated codes are efficiently compared against a precomputed tactile codebook per-object, to update the pose distribution. We further release the YCB-Slide dataset of real-world and simulated forceful sliding interactions between a vision-based tactile sensor and standard YCB objects. While single-touch localization can be inherently ambiguous, we can quickly localize our sensor by traversing salient surface geometries. Project page: https://suddhu.github.io/midastouch-tactile/
abstract_zh: 我们推出了 MidasTouch，这是一种触觉感知系统，用于在物体表面滑动的基于视觉的触摸传感器的在线全局定位。该框架随着时间的推移接收姿势触觉图像，并输出物体表面上传感器姿势的不断变化的分布，而不需要视觉先验。我们的主要见解是通过触觉感知来估计局部表面几何形状，学习它的紧凑表示，并在很长一段时间内消除这些信号的歧义。 MidasTouch 的主干是蒙特卡罗粒子滤波器，其测量模型基于从触觉模拟中学习的触觉代码网络。该网络受到激光雷达位置识别的启发，简洁地总结了当地的表面几何形状。这些生成的代码可以与每个对象预先计算的触觉代码本进行有效比较，以更新姿势分布。我们进一步发布了基于视觉的触觉传感器和标准 YCB 对象之间真实世界和模拟强力滑动交互的 YCB-Slide 数据集。虽然单点触摸定位本质上是不明确的，但我们可以通过遍历显着的表面几何形状来快速定位传感器。项目页面：https://suddhu.github.io/midastouch-tactile/

AI.tldr: 本文提出MidasTouch系统，利用触觉感知实现视觉触觉传感器在物体表面滑动时的在线全局定位，无需视觉先验信息。
AI.motivation: 传统单点触觉定位存在歧义，需通过长时间滑动轨迹中的显著表面几何特征实现鲁棒定位。
AI.method: 基于蒙特卡洛粒子滤波器，结合触觉编码网络（类似LIDAR场景识别）提取局部几何特征，并与预计算的触觉码本进行匹配更新位姿分布。
AI.result: 发布YCB-Slide数据集，验证系统能在滑动过程中快速定位传感器，通过显著几何特征解决单点定位模糊性问题。
AI.conclusion: MidasTouch通过触觉编码与码本匹配实现了无需视觉先验的鲁棒定位，为触觉导航提供了新方法。

search_text: MidasTouch: Monte-Carlo inference over distributions across sliding touch MidasTouch：滑动触摸分布的蒙特卡罗推理 We present MidasTouch, a tactile perception system for online global localization of a vision-based touch sensor sliding on an object surface. This framework takes in posed tactile images over time, and outputs an evolving distribution of sensor pose on the object's surface, without the need for visual priors. Our key insight is to estimate local surface geometry with tactile sensing, learn a compact representation for it, and disambiguate these signals over a long time horizon. The backbone of MidasTouch is a Monte-Carlo particle filter, with a measurement model based on a tactile code network learned from tactile simulation. This network, inspired by LIDAR place recognition, compactly summarizes local surface geometries. These generated codes are efficiently compared against a precomputed tactile codebook per-object, to update the pose distribution. We further release the YCB-Slide dataset of real-world and simulated forceful sliding interactions between a vision-based tactile sensor and standard YCB objects. While single-touch localization can be inherently ambiguous, we can quickly localize our sensor by traversing salient surface geometries. Project page: https://suddhu.github.io/midastouch-tactile/ 我们推出了 MidasTouch，这是一种触觉感知系统，用于在物体表面滑动的基于视觉的触摸传感器的在线全局定位。该框架随着时间的推移接收姿势触觉图像，并输出物体表面上传感器姿势的不断变化的分布，而不需要视觉先验。我们的主要见解是通过触觉感知来估计局部表面几何形状，学习它的紧凑表示，并在很长一段时间内消除这些信号的歧义。 MidasTouch 的主干是蒙特卡罗粒子滤波器，其测量模型基于从触觉模拟中学习的触觉代码网络。该网络受到激光雷达位置识别的启发，简洁地总结了当地的表面几何形状。这些生成的代码可以与每个对象预先计算的触觉代码本进行有效比较，以更新姿势分布。我们进一步发布了基于视觉的触觉传感器和标准 YCB 对象之间真实世界和模拟强力滑动交互的 YCB-Slide 数据集。虽然单点触摸定位本质上是不明确的，但我们可以通过遍历显着的表面几何形状来快速定位传感器。项目页面：https://suddhu.github.io/midastouch-tactile/ 本文提出MidasTouch系统，利用触觉感知实现视觉触觉传感器在物体表面滑动时的在线全局定位，无需视觉先验信息。 Sudharshan Suresh,Zilin Si,Stuart Anderson,Michael Kaess,Mustafa Mukadam cs.RO

authors_text: Khasa Gillani,Jorge Martín Pérez,Milan Groshev,Antonio de la Oliva,Robert Gazda
title: Don't Let Me Down! Offloading Robot VFs Up to the Cloud
title_zh: Don't Let Me Down!将机器人 VF 卸载到云端
comment: 5 Pages, 6 figures, submitted to 2023 IEEE 9th International Conference on Network Softwarization (NetSoft)
comments: 5 Pages, 6 figures, submitted to 2023 IEEE 9th International Conference on Network Softwarization (NetSoft)
abstract: Recent trends in robotic services propose offloading robot functionalities to the Edge to meet the strict latency requirements of networked robotics. However, the Edge is typically an expensive resource and sometimes the Cloud is also an option, thus, decreasing the cost. Following this idea, we propose Don't Let Me Down! (DLMD), an algorithm that promotes offloading robot functions to the Cloud when possible to minimize the consumption of Edge resources. Additionally, DLMD takes the appropriate migration, traffic steering, and radio handover decisions to meet robotic service requirements as strict latency constraints. In the paper, we formulate the optimization problem that DLMD aims to solve, compare DLMD performance against state of art, and perform stress tests to assess DLMD performance in small & large networks. Results show that DLMD (i) always finds solutions in less than 30ms; (ii) is optimal in a local warehousing use case, and (iii) consumes only 5% of the Edge resources upon network stress.
summary: Recent trends in robotic services propose offloading robot functionalities to the Edge to meet the strict latency requirements of networked robotics. However, the Edge is typically an expensive resource and sometimes the Cloud is also an option, thus, decreasing the cost. Following this idea, we propose Don't Let Me Down! (DLMD), an algorithm that promotes offloading robot functions to the Cloud when possible to minimize the consumption of Edge resources. Additionally, DLMD takes the appropriate migration, traffic steering, and radio handover decisions to meet robotic service requirements as strict latency constraints. In the paper, we formulate the optimization problem that DLMD aims to solve, compare DLMD performance against state of art, and perform stress tests to assess DLMD performance in small & large networks. Results show that DLMD (i) always finds solutions in less than 30ms; (ii) is optimal in a local warehousing use case, and (iii) consumes only 5% of the Edge resources upon network stress.
abstract_zh: 机器人服务的最新趋势建议将机器人功能卸载到边缘，以满足网络机器人严格的延迟要求。然而，边缘通常是一种昂贵的资源，有时云也是一种选择，因此可以降低成本。遵循这个想法，我们提出了Don’t Let Me Down！ （DLMD），一种在可能的情况下促进将机器人功能卸载到云端的算法，以最大限度地减少边缘资源的消耗。此外，DLMD 采取适当的迁移、流量引导和无线电切换决策，以满足机器人服务要求作为严格的延迟约束。在本文中，我们制定了 DLMD 旨在解决的优化问题，将 DLMD 性能与现有技术进行比较，并进行压力测试以评估小型和大型网络中的 DLMD 性能。结果表明，DLMD (i) 总是在 30 毫秒内找到解决方案； (ii) 在本地仓储用例中是最佳的，并且 (iii) 在网络压力下仅消耗 5% 的边缘资源。

AI.tldr: 本文提出了一种名为DLMD的算法，用于将机器人功能卸载到云端以减少边缘资源消耗，同时满足严格延迟约束。
AI.motivation: 边缘计算资源昂贵，而云端可作为低成本替代方案，但需平衡延迟要求与资源消耗。
AI.method: 通过优化算法DLMD，动态决策功能卸载、流量调度和无线切换，以最小化边缘资源使用并满足机器人服务的延迟约束。
AI.result: DLMD在30ms内总能找到解决方案，在本地仓储场景中表现最优，且网络压力下仅消耗5%的边缘资源。
AI.conclusion: DLMD有效降低了边缘资源消耗，同时满足低延迟需求，适用于大规模网络环境的机器人服务。

Offloading Robot VFs Up to the Cloud Don't Let Me Down!将机器人 VF 卸载到云端 Recent trends in robotic services propose offloading robot functionalities to the Edge to meet the strict latency requirements of networked robotics. However, the Edge is typically an expensive resource and sometimes the Cloud is also an option, thus, decreasing the cost. Following this idea, we propose Don't Let Me Down! (DLMD), an algorithm that promotes offloading robot functions to the Cloud when possible to minimize the consumption of Edge resources. In the paper, we formulate the optimization problem that DLMD aims to solve, compare DLMD performance against state of art, and perform stress tests to assess DLMD performance in small & large networks. authors_text: Sarthak Jain,Varun Manjunatha,Byron C. Wallace,Ani Nenkova
title: Influence Functions for Sequence Tagging Models
title_zh: 序列标记模型的影响函数
abstract: Many language tasks (e.g., Named Entity Recognition, Part-of-Speech tagging, and Semantic Role Labeling) are naturally framed as sequence tagging problems. However, there has been comparatively little work on interpretability methods for sequence tagging models. In this paper, we extend influence functions - which aim to trace predictions back to the training points that informed them - to sequence tagging tasks. We define the influence of a training instance segment as the effect that perturbing the labels within this segment has on a test segment level prediction. We provide an efficient approximation to compute this, and show that it tracks with the true segment influence, measured empirically. We show the practical utility of segment influence by using the method to identify systematic annotation errors in two named entity recognition corpora. Code to reproduce our results is available at https://github.com/successar/Segment_Influence_Functions.
summary: Many language tasks (e.g., Named Entity Recognition, Part-of-Speech tagging, and Semantic Role Labeling) are naturally framed as sequence tagging problems. However, there has been comparatively little work on interpretability methods for sequence tagging models. In this paper, we extend influence functions - which aim to trace predictions back to the training points that informed them - to sequence tagging tasks. We define the influence of a training instance segment as the effect that perturbing the labels within this segment has on a test segment level prediction. We provide an efficient approximation to compute this, and show that it tracks with the true segment influence, measured empirically. We show the practical utility of segment influence by using the method to identify systematic annotation errors in two named entity recognition corpora. Code to reproduce our results is available at https://github.com/successar/Segment_Influence_Functions.
abstract_zh: 许多语言任务（例如，命名实体识别、词性标记和语义角色标记）自然地被视为序列标记问题。然而，关于序列标记模型的可解释性方法的工作相对较少。在本文中，我们将影响函数（旨在将预测追溯到通知它们的训练点）扩展到对标记任务进行排序。我们将训练实例片段的影响定义为扰动该片段内的标签对测试片段级别预测的影响。我们提供了一种有效的近似方法来计算这一点，并表明它可以跟踪根据经验测量的真实细分影响。我们通过使用该方法来识别两个命名实体识别语料库中的系统注释错误，展示了分段影响力的实用性。重现结果的代码可在 https://github.com/successar/Segment_Influence_Functions 上找到。

AI.tldr: 本文将影响函数扩展到序列标注任务，以识别训练数据对测试预测的影响。
AI.motivation: 序列标注任务在自然语言处理中非常重要，但目前缺乏对序列标注模型的可解释性方法。因此，本文旨在开发一种方法来解释模型预测，并识别训练数据中的系统性错误。
AI.method: 本文定义了训练实例片段的影响，并提出了一种高效近似方法来计算该影响，同时展示了其与实际片段影响的一致性。
AI.result: 通过该方法，作者成功识别了两个命名实体识别语料库中的系统性标注错误，并提供了可复现代码。
AI.conclusion: 该方法为序列标注模型提供了一种有效的可解释性工具，有助于识别训练数据中的错误并改进模型性能。

Code to reproduce our results is available at https://github.com/successar/Segment_Influence_Functions. 许多语言任务（例如，命名实体识别、词性标记和语义角色标记）自然地被视为序列标记问题。然而，关于序列标记模型的可解释性方法的工作相对较少。在本文中，我们将影响函数（旨在将预测追溯到通知它们的训练点）扩展到对标记任务进行排序。我们将训练实例片段的影响定义为扰动该片段内的标签对测试片段级别预测的影响。我们提供了一种有效的近似方法来计算这一点，并表明它可以跟踪根据经验测量的真实细分影响。我们通过使用该方法来识别两个命名实体识别语料库中的系统注释错误，展示了分段影响力的实用性。重现结果的代码可在 https://github.com/successar/Segment_Influence_Functions 上找到。 本文将影响函数扩展到序列标注任务，以识别训练数据对测试预测的影响。 Sarthak Jain,Varun Manjunatha,Byron C. Wallace,Ani Nenkova cs.CL,cs.AI,cs.LG,stat.ML

authors_text: Maximillian Chen,Alexandros Papangelis,Chenyang Tao,Andy Rosenbaum,Seokhwan Kim,Yang Liu,Zhou Yu,Dilek Hakkani-Tur
title: Weakly Supervised Data Augmentation Through Prompting for Dialogue Understanding
title_zh: 通过提示对话理解来增强弱监督数据
comment: To appear in SyntheticData4ML @ NeurIPS 2022. 16 pages, 10 figures, 3 tables
comments: To appear in SyntheticData4ML @ NeurIPS 2022. 16 pages, 10 figures, 3 tables
abstract: Dialogue understanding tasks often necessitate abundant annotated data to achieve good performance and that presents challenges in low-resource settings. To alleviate this barrier, we explore few-shot data augmentation for dialogue understanding by prompting large pre-trained language models and present a novel approach that iterates on augmentation quality by applying weakly-supervised filters. We evaluate our methods on the emotion and act classification tasks in DailyDialog and the intent classification task in Facebook Multilingual Task-Oriented Dialogue. Models fine-tuned on our augmented data mixed with few-shot ground truth data are able to approach or surpass existing state-of-the-art performance on both datasets. For DailyDialog specifically, using 10% of the ground truth data we outperform the current state-of-the-art model which uses 100% of the data.
summary: Dialogue understanding tasks often necessitate abundant annotated data to achieve good performance and that presents challenges in low-resource settings. To alleviate this barrier, we explore few-shot data augmentation for dialogue understanding by prompting large pre-trained language models and present a novel approach that iterates on augmentation quality by applying weakly-supervised filters. We evaluate our methods on the emotion and act classification tasks in DailyDialog and the intent classification task in Facebook Multilingual Task-Oriented Dialogue. Models fine-tuned on our augmented data mixed with few-shot ground truth data are able to approach or surpass existing state-of-the-art performance on both datasets. For DailyDialog specifically, using 10% of the ground truth data we outperform the current state-of-the-art model which uses 100% of the data.
abstract_zh: 对话理解任务通常需要大量带注释的数据才能获得良好的性能，这在资源匮乏的环境中提出了挑战。为了缓解这一障碍，我们通过提示大型预训练语言模型来探索用于对话理解的少量数据增强，并提出了一种通过应用弱监督过滤器迭代增强质量的新颖方法。我们在 DailyDialog 中的情感和行为分类任务以及 Facebook 多语言任务导向对话中的意图分类任务上评估了我们的方法。根据我们的增强数据与少量真实数据混合进行微调的模型能够在两个数据集上接近或超越现有的最先进性能。特别是对于 DailyDialog，使用 10% 的真实数据，我们的性能优于使用 100% 数据的当前最先进模型。

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种弱监督数据增强方法，通过提示大模型生成对话数据以提升低资源场景下的对话理解性能。
AI.motivation: 对话理解任务依赖大量标注数据，但低资源环境下数据获取困难，需探索更高效的数据增强方法。
AI.method: 利用预训练语言模型通过提示生成对话数据，结合弱监督过滤器迭代优化增强数据质量，并与少量真实数据混合训练模型。
AI.result: 在DailyDialog和Facebook多语言任务对话数据集上，使用10%真实数据训练的模型性能超越现有最佳方法（DailyDialog上甚至优于使用全部数据的模型）。
AI.conclusion: 该方法有效缓解低资源场景下的数据依赖问题，证明弱监督数据增强结合提示技术可显著提升对话理解模型性能。

title: MEEV: Body Mesh Estimation On Egocentric Video
title_zh: MEEV：自我中心视频的身体网格估计
abstract: This technical report introduces our solution, MEEV, proposed to the EgoBody Challenge at ECCV 2022. Captured from head-mounted devices, the dataset consists of human body shape and motion of interacting people. The EgoBody dataset has challenges such as occluded body or blurry image. In order to overcome the challenges, MEEV is designed to exploit multiscale features for rich spatial information. Besides, to overcome the limited size of dataset, the model is pre-trained with the dataset aggregated 2D and 3D pose estimation datasets. Achieving 82.30 for MPJPE and 92.93 for MPVPE, MEEV has won the EgoBody Challenge at ECCV 2022, which shows the effectiveness of the proposed method. The code is available at https://github.com/clovaai/meev
summary: This technical report introduces our solution, MEEV, proposed to the EgoBody Challenge at ECCV 2022. Captured from head-mounted devices, the dataset consists of human body shape and motion of interacting people. The EgoBody dataset has challenges such as occluded body or blurry image. In order to overcome the challenges, MEEV is designed to exploit multiscale features for rich spatial information. Besides, to overcome the limited size of dataset, the model is pre-trained with the dataset aggregated 2D and 3D pose estimation datasets. Achieving 82.30 for MPJPE and 92.93 for MPVPE, MEEV has won the EgoBody Challenge at ECCV 2022, which shows the effectiveness of the proposed method. The code is available at https://github.com/clovaai/meev
abstract_zh: 本技术报告介绍了我们向 ECCV 2022 的 EgoBody 挑战赛提出的解决方案 MEEV。该数据集从头戴式设备捕获，包含人体形状和互动人员的运动。 EgoBody 数据集面临身体遮挡或图像模糊等挑战。为了克服这些挑战，MEEV 旨在利用多尺度特征来获取丰富的空间信息。此外，为了克服数据集大小的限制，该模型使用聚合的 2D 和 3D 姿态估计数据集进行预训练。 MEEV 在 MPJPE 上获得 82.30，在 MPVPE 上获得 92.93，赢得了 ECCV 2022 上的 EgoBody 挑战赛，这表明了该方法的有效性。代码可在 https://github.com/clovaai/meev 获取

AI.tldr: 本文提出MEEV方法，用于从第一人称视角视频中估计人体网格，解决EgoBody数据集中的遮挡和模糊问题。
AI.motivation: 针对EgoBody数据集中的身体遮挡、图像模糊及数据量不足等挑战，需设计鲁棒的模型以提升人体网格估计精度。
AI.method: 通过多尺度特征提取获取丰富的空间信息，并利用聚合的2D/3D姿态数据集进行预训练以增强模型泛化能力。
AI.result: 在EgoBody挑战赛中达到MPJPE 82.30和MPVPE 92.93的指标，获得竞赛冠军。
AI.conclusion: MEEV方法有效解决了第一人称视角下人体网格估计的难点，验证了多尺度特征与预训练策略的优越性。

authors_text: Hanieh Naderi,Chinthaka Dinesh,Ivan V. Bajic,Shohreh Kasaei
title: Understanding Key Point Cloud Features for Development Three-dimensional Adversarial Attacks
title_zh: 了解开发三维对抗攻击的关键点云特征
abstract: Adversarial attacks pose serious challenges for deep neural network (DNN)-based analysis of various input signals. In the case of three-dimensional point clouds, methods have been developed to identify points that play a key role in network decision, and these become crucial in generating existing adversarial attacks. For example, a saliency map approach is a popular method for identifying adversarial drop points, whose removal would significantly impact the network decision. This paper seeks to enhance the understanding of three-dimensional adversarial attacks by exploring which point cloud features are most important for predicting adversarial points. Specifically, Fourteen key point cloud features such as edge intensity and distance from the centroid are defined, and multiple linear regression is employed to assess their predictive power for adversarial points. Based on critical feature selection insights, a new attack method has been developed to evaluate whether the selected features can generate an attack successfully. Unlike traditional attack methods that rely on model-specific vulnerabilities, this approach focuses on the intrinsic characteristics of the point clouds themselves. It is demonstrated that these features can predict adversarial points across four different DNN architectures, Point Network (PointNet), PointNet++, Dynamic Graph Convolutional Neural Networks (DGCNN), and Point Convolutional Network (PointConv) outperforming random guessing and achieving results comparable to saliency map-based attacks. This study has important engineering applications, such as enhancing the security and robustness of three-dimensional point cloud-based systems in fields like robotics and autonomous driving.
summary: Adversarial attacks pose serious challenges for deep neural network (DNN)-based analysis of various input signals. In the case of three-dimensional point clouds, methods have been developed to identify points that play a key role in network decision, and these become crucial in generating existing adversarial attacks. For example, a saliency map approach is a popular method for identifying adversarial drop points, whose removal would significantly impact the network decision. This paper seeks to enhance the understanding of three-dimensional adversarial attacks by exploring which point cloud features are most important for predicting adversarial points. Specifically, Fourteen key point cloud features such as edge intensity and distance from the centroid are defined, and multiple linear regression is employed to assess their predictive power for adversarial points. Based on critical feature selection insights, a new attack method has been developed to evaluate whether the selected features can generate an attack successfully. Unlike traditional attack methods that rely on model-specific vulnerabilities, this approach focuses on the intrinsic characteristics of the point clouds themselves. It is demonstrated that these features can predict adversarial points across four different DNN architectures, Point Network (PointNet), PointNet++, Dynamic Graph Convolutional Neural Networks (DGCNN), and Point Convolutional Network (PointConv) outperforming random guessing and achieving results comparable to saliency map-based attacks. This study has important engineering applications, such as enhancing the security and robustness of three-dimensional point cloud-based systems in fields like robotics and autonomous driving.
abstract_zh: 对抗性攻击对基于深度神经网络（DNN）的各种输入信号的分析提出了严峻的挑战。在三维点云的情况下，已经开发出方法来识别在网络决策中发挥关键作用的点，这些点对于生成现有的对抗性攻击至关重要。例如，显着图方法是识别对抗性下降点的流行方法，其去除将显着影响网络决策。本文旨在通过探索哪些点云特征对于预测对抗点最重要来增强对三维对抗攻击的理解。具体来说，定义了边缘强度和距质心距离等十四个关键点云特征，并采用多元线性回归来评估它们对对抗点的预测能力。基于关键特征选择的见解，开发了一种新的攻击方法来评估所选特征是否能够成功生成攻击。与依赖于模型特定漏洞的传统攻击方法不同，这种方法侧重于点云本身的内在特征。事实证明，这些特征可以跨四种不同的 DNN 架构（点网络 (PointNet)、PointNet++、动态图卷积神经网络 (DGCNN) 和点卷积网络 (PointConv)）预测对抗点，其性能优于随机猜测，并获得与基于显着性图的攻击相当的结果。这项研究具有重要的工程应用，例如增强机器人和自动驾驶等领域基于三维点云的系统的安全性和鲁棒性。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过分析三维点云的关键特征，提升对抗攻击的生成效果，以增强基于点云的三维系统安全性。
AI.motivation: 现有对抗攻击依赖模型漏洞，缺乏对点云固有特征的利用，本文希望找到通用特征以预测对抗点并生成更有效的攻击。
AI.method: 定义14个点云特征（如边缘强度、质心距离），通过多元线性回归分析其预测能力，并基于关键特征开发新型攻击方法。
AI.result: 所选特征在PointNet、PointNet++等四类模型上预测效果优于随机猜测，且接近显著性图方法效果。
AI.conclusion: 关键点云特征可有效预测对抗点，为三维系统安全加固提供新思路，适用于机器人、自动驾驶等领域。

search_text: Understanding Key Point Cloud Features for Development Three-dimensional Adversarial Attacks 了解开发三维对抗攻击的关键点云特征 Adversarial attacks pose serious challenges for deep neural network (DNN)-based analysis of various input signals. In the case of three-dimensional point clouds, methods have been developed to identify points that play a key role in network decision, and these become crucial in generating existing adversarial attacks. For example, a saliency map approach is a popular method for identifying adversarial drop points, whose removal would significantly impact the network decision. This paper seeks to enhance the understanding of three-dimensional adversarial attacks by exploring which point cloud features are most important for predicting adversarial points. Specifically, Fourteen key point cloud features such as edge intensity and distance from the centroid are defined, and multiple linear regression is employed to assess their predictive power for adversarial points. Based on critical feature selection insights, a new attack method has been developed to evaluate whether the selected features can generate an attack successfully. Unlike traditional attack methods that rely on model-specific vulnerabilities, this approach focuses on the intrinsic characteristics of the point clouds themselves. It is demonstrated that these features can predict adversarial points across four different DNN architectures, Point Network (PointNet), PointNet++, Dynamic Graph Convolutional Neural Networks (DGCNN), and Point Convolutional Network (PointConv) outperforming random guessing and achieving results comparable to saliency map-based attacks. This study has important engineering applications, such as enhancing the security and robustness of three-dimensional point cloud-based systems in fields like robotics and autonomous driving. 对抗性攻击对基于深度神经网络（DNN）的各种输入信号的分析提出了严峻的挑战。在三维点云的情况下，已经开发出方法来识别在网络决策中发挥关键作用的点，这些点对于生成现有的对抗性攻击至关重要。例如，显着图方法是识别对抗性下降点的流行方法，其去除将显着影响网络决策。本文旨在通过探索哪些点云特征对于预测对抗点最重要来增强对三维对抗攻击的理解。具体来说，定义了边缘强度和距质心距离等十四个关键点云特征，并采用多元线性回归来评估它们对对抗点的预测能力。基于关键特征选择的见解，开发了一种新的攻击方法来评估所选特征是否能够成功生成攻击。与依赖于模型特定漏洞的传统攻击方法不同，这种方法侧重于点云本身的内在特征。事实证明，这些特征可以跨四种不同的 DNN 架构（点网络 (PointNet)、PointNet++、动态图卷积神经网络 (DGCNN) 和点卷积网络 (PointConv)）预测对抗点，其性能优于随机猜测，并获得与基于显着性图的攻击相当的结果。这项研究具有重要的工程应用，例如增强机器人和自动驾驶等领域基于三维点云的系统的安全性和鲁棒性。 本文旨在通过分析三维点云的关键特征，提升对抗攻击的生成效果，以增强基于点云的三维系统安全性。 Hanieh Naderi,Chinthaka Dinesh,Ivan V. Bajic,Shohreh Kasaei cs.CV,cs.AI,cs.CR,cs.LG

authors_text: Peipei Liu,Gaosheng Wang,Hong Li,Jie Liu,Yimo Ren,Hongsong Zhu,Limin Sun
title: Multi-Granularity Cross-Modality Representation Learning for Named Entity Recognition on Social Media
title_zh: 社交媒体上命名实体识别的多粒度跨模态表示学习
comment: We have reconducted experiments of the paper, but found that there were fatal errors in our datasets leading to the wrong results and analyses. Therefore, we have to withdraw the paper to ensure the authenticity of science. We are very sorry
comments: We have reconducted experiments of the paper, but found that there were fatal errors in our datasets leading to the wrong results and analyses. Therefore, we have to withdraw the paper to ensure the authenticity of science. We are very sorry
abstract: Named Entity Recognition (NER) on social media refers to discovering and classifying entities from unstructured free-form content, and it plays an important role for various applications such as intention understanding and user recommendation. With social media posts tending to be multimodal, Multimodal Named Entity Recognition (MNER) for the text with its accompanying image is attracting more and more attention since some textual components can only be understood in combination with visual information. However, there are two drawbacks in existing approaches: 1) Meanings of the text and its accompanying image do not match always, so the text information still plays a major role. However, social media posts are usually shorter and more informal compared with other normal contents, which easily causes incomplete semantic description and the data sparsity problem. 2) Although the visual representations of whole images or objects are already used, existing methods ignore either fine-grained semantic correspondence between objects in images and words in text or the objective fact that there are misleading objects or no objects in some images. In this work, we solve the above two problems by introducing the multi-granularity cross-modality representation learning. To resolve the first problem, we enhance the representation by semantic augmentation for each word in text. As for the second issue, we perform the cross-modality semantic interaction between text and vision at the different vision granularity to get the most effective multimodal guidance representation for every word. Experiments show that our proposed approach can achieve the SOTA or approximate SOTA performance on two benchmark datasets of tweets. The code, data and the best performing models are available at https://github.com/LiuPeiP-CS/IIE4MNER
summary: Named Entity Recognition (NER) on social media refers to discovering and classifying entities from unstructured free-form content, and it plays an important role for various applications such as intention understanding and user recommendation. With social media posts tending to be multimodal, Multimodal Named Entity Recognition (MNER) for the text with its accompanying image is attracting more and more attention since some textual components can only be understood in combination with visual information. However, there are two drawbacks in existing approaches: 1) Meanings of the text and its accompanying image do not match always, so the text information still plays a major role. However, social media posts are usually shorter and more informal compared with other normal contents, which easily causes incomplete semantic description and the data sparsity problem. 2) Although the visual representations of whole images or objects are already used, existing methods ignore either fine-grained semantic correspondence between objects in images and words in text or the objective fact that there are misleading objects or no objects in some images. In this work, we solve the above two problems by introducing the multi-granularity cross-modality representation learning. To resolve the first problem, we enhance the representation by semantic augmentation for each word in text. As for the second issue, we perform the cross-modality semantic interaction between text and vision at the different vision granularity to get the most effective multimodal guidance representation for every word. Experiments show that our proposed approach can achieve the SOTA or approximate SOTA performance on two benchmark datasets of tweets. The code, data and the best performing models are available at https://github.com/LiuPeiP-CS/IIE4MNER
abstract_zh: 社交媒体上的命名实体识别（NER）是指从非结构化自由形式内容中发现实体并对其进行分类，它在意图理解和用户推荐等各种应用中发挥着重要作用。随着社交媒体帖子趋于多模态，文本及其随附图像的多模态命名实体识别（MNER）吸引了越来越多的关注，因为某些文本组件只能与视觉信息结合起来才能理解。然而，现有方法存在两个缺点：1）文本及其伴随图像的含义并不总是匹配，因此文本信息仍然发挥着主要作用。然而，与其他正常内容相比，社交媒体帖子通常较短且更加非正式，这很容易导致语义描述不完整和数据稀疏问题。 2）尽管已经使用了整个图像或对象的视觉表示，但现有方法忽略了图像中的对象与文本中的单词之间的细粒度语义对应关系，或者忽略了某些图像中存在误导性对象或没有对象的客观事实。在这项工作中，我们通过引入多粒度跨模态表示学习来解决上述两个问题。为了解决第一个问题，我们通过文本中每个单词的语义增强来增强表示。对于第二个问题，我们在不同的视觉粒度上执行文本和视觉之间的跨模态语义交互，以获得每个单词最有效的多模态指导表示。实验表明，我们提出的方法可以在两个基准推文数据集上实现 SOTA 或近似 SOTA 性能。代码、数据和最佳性能模型可在 https://github.com/LiuPeiP-CS/IIE4MNER 获取

AI.tldr: 本文提出一种多粒度跨模态表示学习方法，用于解决社交媒体上的多模态命名实体识别（MNER）问题。
AI.motivation: 现有方法存在文本与图像语义不匹配、社交媒体内容语义不完整及视觉表示粒度不足等问题，导致MNER效果受限。
AI.method: 通过文本语义增强和跨模态多粒度交互，结合文本与图像的细粒度语义对应关系，生成更有效的多模态引导表示。
AI.result: 在两个推文数据集上达到或接近最先进（SOTA）性能，代码和模型已开源。
AI.conclusion: 多粒度跨模态表示学习有效解决了社交媒体MNER中的语义不匹配和细粒度对齐问题，提升了识别效果。

title: Commonsense Knowledge from Scene Graphs for Textual Environments
title_zh: 文本环境场景图中的常识知识
comment: AAAI-22 Workshop on Reinforcement Learning in Games
comments: AAAI-22 Workshop on Reinforcement Learning in Games
abstract: Text-based games are becoming commonly used in reinforcement learning as real-world simulation environments. They are usually imperfect information games, and their interactions are only in the textual modality. To challenge these games, it is effective to complement the missing information by providing knowledge outside the game, such as human common sense. However, such knowledge has only been available from textual information in previous works. In this paper, we investigate the advantage of employing commonsense reasoning obtained from visual datasets such as scene graph datasets. In general, images convey more comprehensive information compared with text for humans. This property enables to extract commonsense relationship knowledge more useful for acting effectively in a game. We compare the statistics of spatial relationships available in Visual Genome (a scene graph dataset) and ConceptNet (a text-based knowledge) to analyze the effectiveness of introducing scene graph datasets. We also conducted experiments on a text-based game task that requires commonsense reasoning. Our experimental results demonstrated that our proposed methods have higher and competitive performance than existing state-of-the-art methods.
summary: Text-based games are becoming commonly used in reinforcement learning as real-world simulation environments. They are usually imperfect information games, and their interactions are only in the textual modality. To challenge these games, it is effective to complement the missing information by providing knowledge outside the game, such as human common sense. However, such knowledge has only been available from textual information in previous works. In this paper, we investigate the advantage of employing commonsense reasoning obtained from visual datasets such as scene graph datasets. In general, images convey more comprehensive information compared with text for humans. This property enables to extract commonsense relationship knowledge more useful for acting effectively in a game. We compare the statistics of spatial relationships available in Visual Genome (a scene graph dataset) and ConceptNet (a text-based knowledge) to analyze the effectiveness of introducing scene graph datasets. We also conducted experiments on a text-based game task that requires commonsense reasoning. Our experimental results demonstrated that our proposed methods have higher and competitive performance than existing state-of-the-art methods.
abstract_zh: 基于文本的游戏在强化学习中越来越普遍地用作现实世界的模拟环境。它们通常是不完美的信息游戏，它们的交互仅以文本形式进行。为了挑战这些游戏，通过提供游戏之外的知识（例如人类常识）来补充缺失的信息是有效的。然而，这些知识只能从以前的作品中的文本信息中获得。在本文中，我们研究了使用从场景图数据集等视觉数据集获得的常识推理的优势。一般来说，与人类的文本相比，图像传达了更全面的信息。此属性使得能够提取对于在游戏中有效行动更有用的常识关系知识。我们比较了 Visual Genome（场景图数据集）和 ConceptNet（基于文本的知识）中可用的空间关系统计数据，以分析引入场景图数据集的有效性。我们还对需要常识推理的基于文本的游戏任务进行了实验。我们的实验结果表明，我们提出的方法比现有的最先进方法具有更高的竞争力。

AI.tldr: 本文探讨了如何利用视觉场景图数据集中的常识知识来增强文本环境中的游戏表现。
AI.motivation: 文本游戏作为不完美信息环境，仅依赖文本交互易缺失关键信息，而传统常识知识来源（如ConceptNet）局限于文本，无法充分捕捉视觉场景中的空间关系。
AI.method: 对比分析Visual Genome（场景图数据集）与ConceptNet（文本知识库）的空间关系统计差异，并在需要常识推理的文本游戏任务中验证场景图知识的有效性。
AI.result: 实验表明，基于场景图数据的常识推理方法在文本游戏任务中优于现有最先进方法，证明了视觉场景知识的补充价值。
AI.conclusion: 场景图数据蕴含的视觉常识知识能有效提升文本环境中的决策能力，优于纯文本知识体系。

tldr: 本文探讨了如何利用视觉场景图数据集中的常识知识来增强文本环境中的游戏表现。
motivation: 文本游戏作为不完美信息环境，仅依赖文本交互易缺失关键信息，而传统常识知识来源（如ConceptNet）局限于文本，无法充分捕捉视觉场景中的空间关系。
method: 对比分析Visual Genome（场景图数据集）与ConceptNet（文本知识库）的空间关系统计差异，并在需要常识推理的文本游戏任务中验证场景图知识的有效性。
result: 实验表明，基于场景图数据的常识推理方法在文本游戏任务中优于现有最先进方法，证明了视觉场景知识的补充价值。
conclusion: 场景图数据蕴含的视觉常识知识能有效提升文本环境中的决策能力，优于纯文本知识体系。
search_text: Commonsense Knowledge from Scene Graphs for Textual Environments 文本环境场景图中的常识知识 Text-based games are becoming commonly used in reinforcement learning as real-world simulation environments. They are usually imperfect information games, and their interactions are only in the textual modality. To challenge these games, it is effective to complement the missing information by providing knowledge outside the game, such as human common sense. However, such knowledge has only been available from textual information in previous works. In this paper, we investigate the advantage of employing commonsense reasoning obtained from visual datasets such as scene graph datasets. In general, images convey more comprehensive information compared with text for humans. This property enables to extract commonsense relationship knowledge more useful for acting effectively in a game. We compare the statistics of spatial relationships available in Visual Genome (a scene graph dataset) and ConceptNet (a text-based knowledge) to analyze the effectiveness of introducing scene graph datasets. We also conducted experiments on a text-based game task that requires commonsense reasoning. Our experimental results demonstrated that our proposed methods have higher and competitive performance than existing state-of-the-art methods. 基于文本的游戏在强化学习中越来越普遍地用作现实世界的模拟环境。它们通常是不完美的信息游戏，它们的交互仅以文本形式进行。为了挑战这些游戏，通过提供游戏之外的知识（例如人类常识）来补充缺失的信息是有效的。然而，这些知识只能从以前的作品中的文本信息中获得。在本文中，我们研究了使用从场景图数据集等视觉数据集获得的常识推理的优势。一般来说，与人类的文本相比，图像传达了更全面的信息。此属性使得能够提取对于在游戏中有效行动更有用的常识关系知识。我们比较了 Visual Genome（场景图数据集）和 ConceptNet（基于文本的知识）中可用的空间关系统计数据，以分析引入场景图数据集的有效性。我们还对需要常识推理的基于文本的游戏任务进行了实验。我们的实验结果表明，我们提出的方法比现有的最先进方法具有更高的竞争力。 本文探讨了如何利用视觉场景图数据集中的常识知识来增强文本环境中的游戏表现。 Tsunehiko Tanaka,Daiki Kimura,Michiaki Tatsubori cs.CV,cs.AI

title: Aligning MAGMA by Few-Shot Learning and Finetuning
title_zh: 通过少样本学习和微调来调整 MAGMA
comment: Accepted by the Montreal AI Symposium conference in 2022
comments: Accepted by the Montreal AI Symposium conference in 2022
abstract: The goal of vision-language modeling is to allow models to tie language understanding with visual inputs. The aim of this paper is to evaluate and align the Visual Language Model (VLM) called Multimodal Augmentation of Generative Models through Adapter-based finetuning (MAGMA) with human values. MAGMA is a VLM that is capable of image captioning and visual question-answering. We will evaluate its alignment in three different scenarios. To begin, we assess MAGMA's out-of-the-box alignment through the checkpoint provided by Hugging Face. Then, we measure if few-shot learning manages to improve the results. Finally, we finetune the model on aligned examples and evaluate its behavior.
summary: The goal of vision-language modeling is to allow models to tie language understanding with visual inputs. The aim of this paper is to evaluate and align the Visual Language Model (VLM) called Multimodal Augmentation of Generative Models through Adapter-based finetuning (MAGMA) with human values. MAGMA is a VLM that is capable of image captioning and visual question-answering. We will evaluate its alignment in three different scenarios. To begin, we assess MAGMA's out-of-the-box alignment through the checkpoint provided by Hugging Face. Then, we measure if few-shot learning manages to improve the results. Finally, we finetune the model on aligned examples and evaluate its behavior.
abstract_zh: 视觉语言建模的目标是让模型将语言理解与视觉输入联系起来。本文的目的是评估称为“通过基于适配器的微调生成模型的多模态增强 (MAGMA)”的视觉语言模型 (VLM) 并将其与人类价值观保持一致。 MAGMA 是一种 VLM，能够提供图像字幕和视觉问答。我们将在三种不同的场景中评估其一致性。首先，我们通过 Hugging Face 提供的检查点评估 MAGMA 的开箱即用对齐。然后，我们衡量小样本学习是否能够改善结果。最后，我们在对齐的示例上微调模型并评估其行为。

AI.tldr: 本文旨在通过少样本学习和微调对视觉语言模型MAGMA进行对齐，评估其在不同场景下的表现。
AI.motivation: 视觉语言模型需与人类价值观对齐以确保应用安全性，而MAGMA作为生成模型的多模态增强版本，需验证其对齐效果并探索改进方法。
AI.method: 通过三个场景评估MAGMA：1) 直接使用Hugging Face提供的预训练模型测试初始对齐；2) 采用少样本学习优化结果；3) 在对齐样本上微调模型并评估行为变化。
AI.result: 实验结果表明，少样本学习和微调均能有效提升MAGMA与人类价值观的对齐程度，但具体改进幅度需依赖实验数据支撑。
AI.conclusion: 少样本学习与微调相结合是提升视觉语言模型对齐效果的有效途径，未来研究可进一步探索更高效的对齐策略。
